expected_decision: Refunds under $50: auto-approve. Refunds $50-$200: manager approval required. Over $200: escalate to director.

query: Customer reports product arrived damaged. What are the steps to process this claim?
expected_decision: 1) Verify order and delivery confirmation. 2) Request photos (unless VIP customer). 3) Assess damage category (A/B/C). 4) Check inventory for replacement availability. 5) Offer replacement (expedited, no charge) or refund. 6) File carrier claim if Category B or C and shipment value >$50. 7) Issue 10-15% discount code for inconvenience.
reasoning: Multi-step SOP retrieval with decision tree navigation

query: Do we ship to Canada? What is the cost and timeline?
expected_decision: Yes, we ship to Canada. Cost is $15.95 flat rate (free on orders $100+). Timeline is 7-10 business days. Customer is responsible for customs duties and taxes.

expected_decision: Expedited shipping is $12.95 flat rate with 2-3 business day delivery. Orders placed before 2 PM ET ship same day.

query: Customer received wrong item. They already opened it thinking it was correct. What do we offer?

query: What photo documentation is required for a damaged shipment claim?

reasoning: Documentation requirement lookup with VIP exception handling

query: Customer has filed 3 damage claims in the last 6 months. They are now reporting another damaged shipment. How do we handle this?
expected_decision: Red flag for potential fraud (>2 damage claims in 6 months). Do NOT accuse customer. Process current claim normally but: 1) Photo documentation mandatory, 2) Escalate to manager before proceeding, 3) Flag account for future monitoring, 4) If pattern continues, future damage claims require manager approval.
reasoning: Fraud prevention procedure with escalation, balances customer service with risk management

query: Which cleanser should I recommend for oily, acne-prone skin?

reasoning: Personalized product recommendation based on skin type

query: Customer says their Vitamin C serum turned brown. What do we tell them?
expected_decision: Vitamin C oxidation due to air/light exposure. If slight color change: Still effective but use within 3 months. If dark brown: Product has oxidized - offer replacement. Storage tip: Keep in cool, dark place; refrigeration extends shelf life. Vitamin C products have 6-month shelf life after opening.
reasoning: Product quality issue troubleshooting with replacement criteria

query: Can I use AHA/BHA Exfoliating Toner and Retinol Night Serum on the same night?
expected_decision: Use with caution. Initial recommendation: Alternate nights (AHA/BHA one night, Retinol next night). High sensitivity risk when combined. Advanced users can layer both after 4-6 weeks of building retinol tolerance. Start slow to avoid over-exfoliation.
reasoning: Product compatibility with usage frequency guidance and risk assessment

query: Which sunscreen doesn't leave a white cast?
expected_decision: Tinted Sunscreen SPF 45 has no white cast and is cosmetically elegant. Good for oily skin. Mineral Sunscreen SPF 50 has slight white cast and thicker texture but is best for sensitive/acne-prone skin (non-comedogenic).
reasoning: Product differentiation based on cosmetic properties

query: Customer reports severe irritation and burning from Retinol Night Serum. What do we recommend?
expected_decision: 1) Stop using product immediately. 2) This is severe irritation (not normal retinization period). Concentration may be too high. 3) Process full refund per Adverse Reaction policy. 4) Alternative recommendation: Night Cream (0.1% retinol, lower concentration) or switch to Peptide Firming Serum (retinol-free anti-aging). 5) Document product name, batch number, reaction type. 6) Flag for product quality review. 7) Customer success team will follow up within 48 hours.
reasoning: Adverse reaction handling requiring product knowledge + policy cross-reference
